_doc: Committed regression baseline for tools/ci/regression_gate.sh. Locks the speed/compression/quality wins so they cannot regress silently. Update ONLY with attended review + fresh warm-median evidence, and record the change in docs/campaign/test_matrix.md.
_method: CATEGORY-regression gate, not a micro-benchmark. The fresh-process warm-median has a +-several-percent noise floor (per-process cold-PSO residual); sub-10% deltas are NOT cleanly resolvable this way (test_matrix.md 2026-06-21). Floors are therefore set ~10-15% below the measured warm median: they catch a lever being silently disabled (e.g. predec OFF = -46.7%) or a quant path regressing, while staying above the noise so the gate does not flap red. For fine (<10%) deltas use a single long warm run, not this gate.

models/Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf.guards: Qwen-3B Q4_K_M weight footprint; a swap to a larger/un-quantized file or a disabled compression default would breach this.

models/rwkv7-g1-04-sft-Q4_K_M.gguf.guards: RWKV-7-0.4B-SFT Q4_K_M footprint (the SSM long-context moat SKU).

qwen3b_short.rationale: predec-OFF = 21.6 tps (-46.7%) is the canonical silent regression this floor must catch; 34.0 sits ~11% below the worst warm median (38.3) and far above the predec-off failure, so it catches category regressions without flapping on noise.

rwkv7_sft_short.rationale: Floor ~13% below the overnight warm median (114.6). Guards the SSM decode path; a kernel/state regression that halved RWKV throughput would breach this.

quality_identity_floors._note: These are argmax-IDENTITY floors for Qwen quality-affecting LEVERS (greedy diff vs the bit-identical default over the adversarial suite in tools/bench/ratios.sh). They are a LEVER-SAFETY gate, NOT an instruct/product-quality gate (that needs chat-templated eval via /v1/chat/completions - see open_risks_and_gates.md R3). A breach means a lever got materially less faithful to the default than when it was measured.

profile_fast.rationale: --profile fast is a known mild quant trade; 0.80 catches a real fidelity collapse without flapping on the 1-2 prompts that legitimately diverge.

f16_kv.rationale: F16_KV quality is high; 0.85 catches a collapse (per-ROW int4-KV was 0%) while leaving headroom for rare prompt-specific divergence.

pending_not_enforced._note: Honestly tracked gaps. These are NOT yet gated; do not read their absence as 'covered'.
pending_not_enforced.serve_decode_tps: RWKV serve throughput (R1b) is ~7.8 tps vs ~119 single-stream (B=8 arena overhead). Add a serve-tps floor AFTER the arena-sizing fix lands, so the recovered throughput is locked.
pending_not_enforced.perplexity_int4kv_pc: Real-model perplexity gate for per-channel int4-KV is the documented ship gate and has never been run (test_matrix.md). Add when the path is wired e2e (open_risks_and_gates.md R2).
pending_not_enforced.instruct_quality: Product instruct-quality (chat-templated, per task class) is not gated here; R3/R5. This gate only protects lever argmax-identity + speed + footprint.
